title: viola arvensis subsp. demetria
intro: Viola arvensis subsp. demetria, also known as the woodland violet or pale field pansy, is a subspecies of the viola arvensis plant, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the violet family. Traditionally, Viola arvensis subsp. demetria has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia. In herbal preparations, Viola arvensis subsp. demetria is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
